name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America Kansas City, Missouri — Advance America is a nationally accredited lender offering payday loans up to $500, installment loans up to $1,0...
description_long:
Advance America is a nationally recognized, fully accredited consumer lending company that has served millions of Americans seeking short-term financial solutions. The company describes itself as one of the most trusted sources for emergency cash and maintains a strong local presence in Jacksonville, including its North Edgewood location in Commonwealth Village. With 200,000+ customer reviews on Google and a 4.9-out-of-5 rating drawn from over 125,401 verified reviews, it has an unusually well-documented customer satisfaction record for this segment of lending.

The Jacksonville location offers three core loan products. Payday Loans are available from $100 to $500 and are designed as two-to-four-week short-term bridges between paychecks. Installment Loans range from $100 to $1,000 and allow repayment in multiple scheduled payments over time, offering larger amounts and longer terms than payday products. Title Loans are available from $2,000 to $25,000 and are offered in partnership with LoanCenter, using the borrower's paid-off vehicle title as collateral. The store also provides Western Union money transfer services. Applications can be submitted online, in-store, or by phone for pre-qualification.

What sets this location apart operationally is its bilingual service — the store explicitly advertises Spanish-language staff, making it accessible to a broader Jacksonville demographic. The multi-channel application process (online, in-person, or phone pre-qualification) adds flexibility. Required documents are clearly stated: government-issued ID, proof of income, a checking account, and a Social Security Number or ITIN. Store hours run Monday through Saturday, with slightly extended Friday morning hours.

Honestly, Advance America occupies the high-cost, short-term end of consumer lending. Payday loans at these amounts carry triple-digit APRs typical of the product category, and the store page does not disclose specific rates or fees — borrowers must call or visit to get terms. Title loans put a paid-off vehicle at risk if repayment fails. These products are appropriate for genuine short-term emergencies where the borrower has a clear repayment plan, but they are expensive relative to credit unions, personal loan lenders, or employer advance programs. Consumers with access to lower-cost alternatives should exhaust those first.
pros: 4.9/5 star rating from 125,401 verified reviews — exceptionally high volume for a storefront lender
Three loan types under one roof: payday, installment, and title loans
Title loans up to $25,000 — far above the typical payday/installment cap
Spanish-language staff available in-store for non-English speakers
Online and in-store applications plus phone pre-qualification — multiple access points
Loan requirements clearly listed upfront (ID, income, checking account, SSN/ITIN)
Referral rewards program available for existing customers
cons: Payday loans capped at $500 — lower ceiling than many online installment lenders
Title loans are processed through a third-party partner (LoanCenter), not directly by Advance America
APR, fees, and repayment terms not disclosed on the store page — requires a call or visit to get actual pricing
Requires a checking account, which excludes fully unbanked borrowers
Closed Sundays, limiting access on weekends for workers with non-traditional schedules
best_for: Borrowers needing $100–$500 cash before their next paycheck with a clear repayment plan
Vehicle owners with a paid-off car title who need $2,000–$25,000 quickly
Spanish-speaking consumers in Jacksonville who prefer bilingual in-person service
Consumers who want to apply online but also have the option to complete the process in-store
diagnosis: Advance America is best for consumers facing a genuine short-term cash emergency who need funds same-day and have a checking account and verifiable income. The main caveat is cost: payday and installment loans in this category carry high APRs not disclosed on the store page, and title loans put your paid-off vehicle at risk — these products are a last resort, not a financial planning tool.
services: Title Loans ($2,000–$25,000 via LoanCenter, vehicle as collateral)

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
